seo.title: Building Nuxt 3 server routes and API layers with Vercel...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Nuxt 3 server routes and API layers with Vercel and Netlify.

content.intro: This guide provides a step-by-step implementation strategy for building a performant Nuxt 3 application with server-side rendering, state management, and AI integration, addressing common pain points for European Vue.js teams.

steps.title: Configure SSR and API Routes
steps.explanation: Set up server-side rendering and create API routes in /server/api. Use useAsyncData for data fetching and ensure proper SSR hydration.
pitfalls: Forgetting to set ssr: true for SSR-optimized routes
Not handling loading states in useAsyncData

steps.title: Implement Pinia with Server-Side State
steps.explanation: Create a Pinia store with server-side data persistence. Use $fetch for API calls and ensure state hydration between client and server.
pitfalls: Storing sensitive data in client-side stores
Not using $fetch for server-side API calls

steps.title: Integrate AI Features with Edge Functions
steps.explanation: Create edge functions for AI processing. Use Nuxt 3's server routes with AI SDKs and ensure proper environment variable management.
pitfalls: Exposing AI API keys in client-side code
Not setting up proper CORS for edge functions

steps.title: Optimize Bundle Size
steps.explanation: Implement code splitting with Nuxt's auto-imports and use tree-shaking for AI SDKs. Analyze bundles with webpack-bundle-analyzer.
pitfalls: Including full AI SDK bundles in client-side code

steps.title: Deploy with Vercel Edge
steps.explanation: Configure Vercel for edge deployment. Set environment variables and optimize build settings for Nuxt 3 SSR.

content.conclusion: By following these steps, developers can create scalable Nuxt 3 applications with optimized SSR, proper state management, and secure AI integration. Validate each stage with performance audits and ensure environment variables are properly managed for production deployment.
